title: 2011. évi CXII. törvény az információs önrendelkezési jogról és az információszabadságról
title_en: Act CXII of 2011 on Informational Self-Determination and Freedom of Information
description: Hungary's primary data protection legislation, implementing Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR). Governs the processing of personal data and access to public-interest data. As amended by Act XXXVIII of 2018 to align with GDPR requirements.

provisions.title: § 1 — Purpose and Scope
provisions.content: The purpose of this Act is to lay down the fundamental rules for the right to informational self-determination and the freedom of information, as well as the enforcement thereof. This Act shall apply to any processing of personal data carried out wholly or partly by automated means, and to the processing otherwise than by automated means of personal data which form part of a filing system or are intended to form part of a filing system, in compliance with the requirements of Regulation (EU) 2016/679 of the European Parliament and of the Council (General Data Protection Regulation, GDPR).

provisions.title: § 2 — Definitions
provisions.content: For the purposes of this Act: (a) 'personal data' means any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person (data subject); a natural person is identifiable where the person can be identified, directly or indirectly, in particular by reference to an identifier such as name, identification number, location data, online identifier, or one or more factors specific to the physical, physiological, genetic, mental, economic, cultural or social identity of that natural person; (b) 'data processing' means any operation or set of operations performed on personal data, including collection, recording, organisation, structuring, storage, adaptation, alteration, retrieval, consultation, use, disclosure by transmission, dissemination, alignment, combination, restriction, erasure and destruction; (c) 'data controller' means the natural or legal person, public authority, agency or other body which, alone or jointly with others, determines the purposes and means of the processing of personal data; (d) 'data processor' means a natural or legal person, public authority, agency or other body which processes personal data on behalf of the controller. These definitions shall be applied consistently with Article 4 of Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR).

provisions.title: § 3 — Principles of Data Processing
provisions.content: Personal data may only be processed for specified, explicit and legitimate purposes. Personal data must be adequate, relevant and limited to what is necessary in relation to the purposes for which they are processed (data minimisation). Personal data must be accurate and, where necessary, kept up to date. Personal data must be kept in a form which permits identification of data subjects for no longer than is necessary for the purposes for which the data are processed (storage limitation). Processing shall be carried out in accordance with the principles of lawfulness, fairness and transparency, as set out in Article 5 of Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR).

provisions.title: § 4 — Lawfulness of Processing
provisions.content: Processing of personal data shall be lawful only where at least one of the following conditions is met: (a) the data subject has given consent to the processing of personal data for one or more specific purposes; (b) processing is necessary for the performance of a contract to which the data subject is party or for taking steps at the request of the data subject prior to entering into a contract; (c) processing is necessary for compliance with a legal obligation to which the controller is subject; (d) processing is necessary in order to protect the vital interests of the data subject or of another natural person; (e) processing is necessary for the performance of a task carried out in the public interest or in the exercise of official authority vested in the controller; (f) processing is necessary for the purposes of the legitimate interests pursued by the controller or by a third party. These grounds correspond to Article 6 of Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR).

provisions.title: § 5 — Consent
provisions.content: Consent of the data subject must be freely given, specific, informed and unambiguous indication of the data subject's wishes by which he or she, by a statement or by a clear affirmative action, signifies agreement to the processing of personal data relating to him or her. Where processing is based on consent, the controller shall be able to demonstrate that the data subject has consented to the processing. The data subject shall have the right to withdraw consent at any time. The withdrawal of consent shall not affect the lawfulness of processing based on consent before its withdrawal.

provisions.title: § 6 — Processing of Special Categories of Personal Data
provisions.content: Processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, genetic data, biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation shall be prohibited unless: (a) the data subject has given explicit consent; (b) processing is necessary for carrying out obligations in the field of employment and social security law; (c) processing relates to personal data which are manifestly made public by the data subject; or (d) processing is necessary for reasons of substantial public interest on the basis of Hungarian law.

provisions.title: § 7 — Rights of the Data Subject
provisions.content: The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller confirmation as to whether personal data concerning him or her are being processed. Where personal data are being processed, the data subject shall have the right of access to the data and the following information: (a) the purposes of the processing; (b) the categories of personal data concerned; (c) the recipients or categories of recipients to whom the data have been or will be disclosed; (d) the envisaged period of storage or the criteria used to determine that period; (e) the existence of the right to request rectification, erasure, or restriction of processing; (f) the right to lodge a complaint with the National Authority for Data Protection and Freedom of Information (NAIH).

provisions.title: § 8 — Right to Rectification and Erasure
provisions.content: The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller without undue delay the rectification of inaccurate personal data. The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller the erasure of personal data without undue delay where: (a) the data are no longer necessary for the purposes for which they were collected; (b) the data subject withdraws consent; (c) the data subject objects to processing and there are no overriding legitimate grounds; (d) the data have been unlawfully processed; (e) the data must be erased for compliance with a legal obligation in Hungarian law or Union law.

provisions.title: § 14 — Data Protection Impact Assessment
provisions.content: Where a type of processing is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the controller shall, prior to the processing, carry out an assessment of the impact of the envisaged processing operations on the protection of personal data. The assessment shall contain at least: (a) a systematic description of the envisaged processing operations and the purposes; (b) an assessment of the necessity and proportionality of the processing; (c) an assessment of the risks to the rights and freedoms of data subjects; (d) the measures envisaged to address the risks, in accordance with Article 35 of Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR).

provisions.title: § 15 — Data Protection Officer
provisions.content: The controller and the processor shall designate a data protection officer where: (a) the processing is carried out by a public authority or body; (b) the core activities of the controller or processor consist of processing operations which require regular and systematic monitoring of data subjects on a large scale; or (c) the core activities of the controller or processor consist of processing of special categories of data on a large scale. The data protection officer shall be designated on the basis of professional qualities and expert knowledge of data protection law and practices, in compliance with Articles 37-39 of Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR).

provisions.title: § 24 — Data Breach Notification
provisions.content: In the case of a personal data breach, the controller shall without undue delay and, where feasible, not later than 72 hours after having become aware of it, notify the personal data breach to the National Authority for Data Protection and Freedom of Information (NAIH). Where the personal data breach is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the controller shall communicate the personal data breach to the data subject without undue delay. The notification shall describe the nature of the breach, the categories and approximate number of data subjects concerned, the name and contact details of the data protection officer, the likely consequences, and the measures taken or proposed to address the breach, in accordance with Articles 33-34 of Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR).

provisions.title: § 25 — International Data Transfers
provisions.content: A transfer of personal data to a third country or international organisation may take place where the European Commission has decided that the third country ensures an adequate level of protection. In the absence of an adequacy decision, a transfer may take place only if the controller or processor has provided appropriate safeguards, including standard contractual clauses adopted by the Commission, binding corporate rules, or other safeguards as set out in Article 46 of Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR). The National Authority for Data Protection and Freedom of Information (NAIH) may authorise specific transfers in accordance with Hungarian law.

provisions.title: § 26 — Freedom of Information — Public Data
provisions.content: Bodies performing public duties shall ensure access to data of public interest and data accessible on public interest grounds. Data of public interest means information or knowledge, irrespective of the manner of its recording, processed by a body performing public duties or by any other person on the basis of law, which does not qualify as personal data. The right to access to data of public interest may only be restricted by law, in the interest of national security, national defence, criminal investigation, or the protection of business secrets.

provisions.title: § 28 — Request for Public Data
provisions.content: A request for public data may be submitted orally, in writing, or electronically. The data controller shall fulfil the request within 15 days of receipt. If the request for data of public interest is refused, the data controller shall inform the requester of the reasons for the refusal within 15 days. The requester may bring an action before the court against the refusal or against the failure to fulfil the request.

provisions.title: § 30 — Proactive Publication Obligations
provisions.content: Bodies performing public duties shall publish electronically, in a manner accessible to all, the data of public interest defined in this Act and in the Annex thereto. The publication obligation extends to: (a) organisational and personnel data; (b) activity-related data; (c) data on financial management; (d) data on contracts. The data must be published in a searchable format, free of charge, without requiring registration.

provisions.title: § 38 — National Authority for Data Protection and Freedom of Information (NAIH)
provisions.content: The National Authority for Data Protection and Freedom of Information (Nemzeti Adatvédelmi és Információszabadság Hatóság, NAIH) is the supervisory authority responsible for monitoring and enforcing the application of data protection and freedom of information legislation. NAIH shall be an independent body with its own budget. NAIH acts as the supervisory authority within the meaning of Article 51 of Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR). The President of NAIH shall be appointed by the President of Hungary upon the proposal of the Prime Minister for a term of nine years.

provisions.title: § 55 — Powers of NAIH
provisions.content: NAIH shall have the following investigative, corrective and advisory powers: (a) conduct investigations into data processing operations; (b) carry out data protection audits; (c) issue warnings to controllers or processors; (d) order the controller or processor to comply with data protection provisions; (e) impose administrative fines; (f) order the suspension of data transfers to third countries; (g) bring matters before the courts. The administrative fines imposed by NAIH may amount to up to twenty million forints, or in respect of an undertaking, up to the equivalent of the amounts prescribed by Article 83 of Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR).

provisions.title: § 60 — Judicial Remedies
provisions.content: The data subject may bring an action before the court against the controller or processor where the data subject considers that the processing of personal data infringes the provisions of this Act or Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR). The action may be brought before the tribunal having jurisdiction at the domicile or habitual residence of the data subject. The controller or processor shall bear the burden of proving that the processing complies with this Act and the GDPR. Any person who has suffered material or non-material damage as a result of an infringement of this Act or the GDPR shall have the right to receive compensation from the controller or processor.

provisions.title: § 65 — Classification of Data
provisions.content: For the purposes of this Act, data shall be classified as: (a) personal data, (b) special categories of personal data, (c) data of public interest, (d) data accessible on public interest grounds. Data of public interest may not be classified as business secrets or other restricted data for the sole purpose of preventing public access. The controller performing public duties must maintain a register of data processing activities and make the register publicly accessible.

provisions.title: § 72 — Transitional and Final Provisions
provisions.content: This Act shall enter into force on 1 January 2012. The provisions of Chapter III on the National Authority for Data Protection and Freedom of Information (NAIH) shall replace the former Act on Data Protection. Data processing operations commenced before the entry into force of this Act shall be brought into compliance within one year. As of 25 May 2018, the provisions of this Act shall be applied in conjunction with Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR), and in the event of conflict between this Act and the GDPR, the provisions of the GDPR shall prevail.

definitions.definition: Any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person (data subject), as defined in Article 4(1) of Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR).
Any operation or set of operations performed on personal data or on sets of personal data, whether or not by automated means.
The natural or legal person, public authority, agency or other body which, alone or jointly with others, determines the purposes and means of the processing of personal data.
Nemzeti Adatvédelmi és Információszabadság Hatóság — the National Authority for Data Protection and Freedom of Information, acting as the supervisory authority under GDPR.
Information or knowledge processed by a body performing public duties which does not qualify as personal data.
